keyMyths.title: Prince Siddhartha's Renunciation
keyMyths.description: Prince Siddhartha, sheltered from suffering, ventures beyond his palace walls and witnesses the realities of old age, sickness, and death, prompting him to renounce his privileged life in search of enlightenment.

keyMyths.title: The Great Departure
keyMyths.description: Siddhartha secretly leaves his palace at night, abandoning his wife and son, to become an ascetic and pursue spiritual liberation from the cycle of rebirth.

keyMyths.title: Mara's Temptation
keyMyths.description: As Siddhartha meditates under the Bodhi tree, Mara, the demon of desire and death, unleashes his armies of temptation and fear to prevent him from attaining enlightenment, but Siddhartha remains steadfast.

keyMyths.title: The First Sermon
keyMyths.description: After achieving enlightenment, the Buddha delivers his first sermon at Sarnath, setting in motion the Wheel of Dharma and teaching the Four Noble Truths and the Eightfold Path.

keyMyths.title: Parinirvana
keyMyths.description: At the age of 80, the Buddha accepts a final meal and enters Parinirvana, the final cessation of rebirth, at Kushinagar, leaving behind his teachings and community of disciples.

achievements: Renouncing royal life and embracing asceticism.
Achieving enlightenment under the Bodhi tree.
Formulating and teaching the Four Noble Truths.
Establishing the Eightfold Path as a means to liberation.
Founding the Sangha, the monastic community.
Spreading the Dharma throughout India and beyond.

sources.name: Buddhacarita
sources.description: An epic poem on the life of the Buddha by Ashvaghosha.
sources.title: Buddhacarita

sources.name: Lalitavistara Sutra
sources.description: A Mahayana Buddhist sutra that recounts the life of the Buddha from his birth to his first sermon.
sources.title: Lalitavistara Sutra

sources.name: Nidanakatha
sources.description: The introductory narrative to the Jataka tales, providing a biography of the Buddha.
sources.title: Nidanakatha

sources.name: Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta
sources.description: The sutra of the Turning of the Wheel of Dharma, the Buddha's first sermon.
sources.title: Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta

sources.name: Mahaparinibbana Sutta
sources.description: The sutra detailing the Buddha's final days, death, and entry into Parinirvana.
sources.title: Mahaparinibbana Sutta

sources.name: Jataka Tales
sources.description: A collection of stories about the previous lives of the Buddha.
sources.title: Jataka Tales
